chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الرَّمْيِ عَنِ الْعَلِيلِ وَ الصِّبْيَانِ وَ الرَّمْيِ رَاكِباً
chapter_title.en: Chapter 177 – The pelting (of the Jamarah) on behalf of the sick, and the children, and the pelting while riding

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/155)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/155)
text: الْكَسِيرُ وَ الْمَبْطُونُ يُرْمَى عَنْهُمَا قَالَ وَ الصِّبْيَانُ يُرْمَى عَنْهُمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya Bin Ammar, and Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The disabled, and the one with stomach illness, (the Jamarah) would be pelted on both their behalf’. Heasws said: ‘And the children, (the Jamarah) would be pelted on their behalf’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar and ‘ Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is permissible to throw pebbles on behalf of one who has a broken part or has stomach illness and so also is the rule about children.’”
